rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Leave To File Against City And County Of San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 2, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER PATRINA HALL's Hearing On Petition For Leave To File Against City And County Of San Francisco. Petitioner Patrina Hall's petition for an order relieving her from her failure to present a timely Government Code claim to respondent City and County of San Francisco is denied. Viewed against the backdrop of her having previously filed two Government Code claims with the CCSF Controller, including one she personally filed shortly before the date of the incident that is the subject of this petition, Ms. Hall's conclusory declaration raises more questions than it answers and falls short of the required showings of (1) mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect and (2) reasonable diligence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: UNITED EDUCATORS OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 2, 2018, Line 2, PETITIONER UNITED EDUCATORS OF SAN FRANCISCO, AFT/CFT, NCA/CTA's Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration. Petitioner United Educators of San Francisco's petition to compel arbitration is granted. The arbitration, however, shall not include the issue whether respondent SFUSD violated Article 5.7 of the Collective Bargaining Agreement. The scope of arbitrability is a question for the court. (AT&T Technologies, Inc. v. Communication Workers of America, 475 U.S. 643, 649 (1986).) Per Articles 19.6, 19.8.3.3.1, and 19.9.2 of the CBA, it is too late to add the Article 5.7 issue to the arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for SFUSD is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents Set Three To Martin Mcnerney Development Inc Dba Martin Building Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents Set Three To Martin Mcnerney Development Inc Dba Martin Building Company. Pro Tem Judge Vedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to August 16, 2018 to give the moving party a chance to comply with SF Local Rule 2.7B. If it has not done so, all parties shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of its filing to Dept. 301 with a cover letter that reflects the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to vpuri@pillsburycoleman.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SOHNI AHLUWALIA VS. ANNE KERR KENNEDY LHEUREUX ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Sohni Ahluwalia'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Terminating Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declarations Of Plaintiff Sohni Ahluwalia And John A. Holman
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 2, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SOHNI AHLUWALIA's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Terminating Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declarations Of Plaintiff Sohni Ahluwalia And John A. Holman. Hearing required. Ms. Ahluwalia's refusal to submit to a further session of her deposition after being ordered to do so and giving varying excuses why she would or could not be deposed, not any actions or inactions of defendants or their counsel, is what resulted in the imposition of terminating sanctions and the failure to have those sanctions vacated. To the extent that this motion is based on asserted misconduct of defendants or their counsel, the motion is misguided. To the extent that the motion is based on Ms. Ahluwalia's claimed mental infirmities as a causal factor for her past intransigence and repeated changing positions and/or her current willingness to submit to a further session of her deposition without casting aspersions on defendants or their counsel, the motion might have possible merit. However, the motion does not appear to be based on those grounds or supported by any evidence establishing the existence of such grounds. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: BELINDA H. ET AL VS. ANTHEM BLUE CROSS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 2, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT ANTHEM BLUE CROSS, REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The demurrer will be heard at 9:30am on August 10, 2018 by Judge Ulmer in department 302.=(302/HK)

Set for hearing on Thursday, August 2, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT COINBASE INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Coinbase, Inc.'s demurrer to all seven causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Darrell Archer is a) overruled as the first, second and third causes of action for conversion, negligence and breach of contract; b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty, intentional tort, and fraud; and c) sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for reckless misconduct. Mr. Archer adequately alleges a conversion claim based on his right to own and possess the 350 Bitcoin Gold coins and Coinbase's interference with that right. Mr. Archer adequately alleges a negligence claim based on a duty of care owed to him by Coinbase with regard to the ownership and possession of the Bitcoin Gold coins. Mr. Archer adequately alleges a breach of contract claim based on Coinbase's breach of an agreement to provide the Bitcoin Gold coins to him. Mr. Archer's breach of fiduciary duty claim fails because he has not alleged facts showing that Coinbase owed him a fiduciary duty. Mr. Archer's intentional tort fails because he has not alleged facts supporting any cognizable tort claim other than conversion. Mr. Archer's fraud claim fails because he has not alleged facts showing that Coinbase made an actionable misrepresentation that he relied on, or made a false promise that he relied on, or concealed any facts that it had a duty to disclose. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Archer has leave to remedy the defects in his breach of fiduciary duty, intentional tort, and fraud claims. Mr. Archer's reckless misconduct claim fails because there is no such claim and it does not appear reasonably possible that Mr. Archer can allege a claim where the scienter required is recklessness other than what he has already alleged or purports to allege. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Coinbase is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

Set for hearing on Thursday, August 2, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT COINBASE INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Coinbase, Inc.'s motion to strike the request for exemplary damages in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Darrell Archer is denied. Mr. Archer sufficiently alleges that, in converting his Bitcoin Gold coins, Coinbase acted with malice and/or oppression as those terms are defined in the punitive damages statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Archer is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)
